---
title: "Machine Learning Summer School 2026"
format:
  html:
    title-block: false
    toc: false
---

::: {.hub-hero}
# Welcome

::: {.hub-tagline}
Slides, **reading companion chapters**, notebooks, and a short map of the week — all in one place.
:::

::: {.hub-lead}
Use this site **during and after class**: open today’s deck, then try the matching notebook or **[lab exercise](exercises/index.qmd)** when you feel like coding along. If something is unclear, that’s normal — bring it to the room or office hours.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-tip .hub-start appearance="simple"}
### <img src="assets/hub-icons/map.svg" class="hub-inline-icon" alt="" aria-hidden="true" /> Start here (2 minutes)

1. **Open today’s slides** — we follow **Day 1 (Monday) → Day 2 (Tuesday) → Day 4 (Thursday)** in class.
2. **When you’re ready**, open a **reading companion chapter** for explanation, or a **notebook** for a long IDE lab — same R you already use (RStudio or Positron is fine).
3. **Skim “Your week”** below for the story of the three days — no jargon quiz attached.
:::

::: {.hub-grid}
::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
<img src="assets/hub-icons/penguin.svg" class="hub-inline-icon" alt="" aria-hidden="true" /> Palmer Penguins
:::
### Our real-data running example

- **[Data catalog](data/index.qmd)** — data cards for every dataset (penguins, genes, Old Faithful, Hald, microbiome)  
- **[Palmer Penguins card](data/cards/palmer-penguins.qmd)** — variables, biology, caveats, teaching slices **Monday → Thursday**  
- Links to **penguin-only** notebooks from that page (mass, binary species, sex, three-class species); `tidymodels` walk-throughs live in **Chapter 4** (+ **7–8** follow-ups; see the [reading companion](modules/index.qmd)).

Synthetic **gene** notebooks are there so we **know the true story behind the data** (which “genes” really matter) while we teach LASSO, ridge, and AIC.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
<img src="assets/hub-icons/slides.svg" class="hub-inline-icon" alt="" aria-hidden="true" /> Class slides
:::
### Follow along day by day

- **[Day 1 (Monday)](slides/day-01-monday.html)** — from questions to simple models: when a straight line (or curve) helps, and when a model is “trying too hard.”  
- **[Day 2 (Tuesday)](slides/day-02-tuesday.html)** — branching decisions (trees), and how to keep your code organized so Day 4 still makes sense.  
- **[Day 4 (Thursday)](slides/day-04-thursday.html)** — forests, boosting, neural nets, PCA/imputation in recipes, variable importance, and evaluation.  

Each deck links back here from the **footer** if you get lost.

**Printable PDF:** open a deck, click **Print PDF** in the footer (or add `?print-pdf` to the URL), then **Save as PDF** in Chrome (Landscape, no margins, background graphics on). See [README — Printable slides](README.md#printable-slides-pdf).
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
<img src="assets/hub-icons/code.svg" class="hub-inline-icon" alt="" aria-hidden="true" /> Hands-on notebooks
:::
### Try the code yourself

- **[Notebook home](notebooks/index.qmd)** — long `.Rmd` labs (**classical** `lm` / `glm` / `rpart` / `glmnet` on genes + penguins)  
- **`tidymodels` on penguins (reading companion):** [Chapter 4](modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd#train-test-last-fit) (train/test, then [Day 2 pipeline](modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd#canonical-pipeline-tuesday)), [Chapter 7](modules/module-07-penguins-choose-metrics.qmd) (metrics), [Chapter 8](modules/module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd) (compare RF / XGB / MLP)  
- **Genes (simulation where we know the answers):** [linear + LASSO](notebooks/linear-regression-lasso.Rmd), [logistic + tree](notebooks/logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd)  
- **Palmer Penguins (notebooks):** [body mass](notebooks/penguins-body-mass.Rmd), [Adelie vs Gentoo](notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd), [sex](notebooks/penguins-sex-classification.Rmd), [three species](notebooks/penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd)  

Go as fast or slow as you like; notebooks and chapters are for **you**, not for a grade on this site.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
<img src="assets/hub-icons/lab.svg" class="hub-inline-icon" alt="" aria-hidden="true" /> Lab exercises
:::
### Afternoon practicals (with solutions)

- **[Lab exercises home](exercises/index.qmd)** — task handbook plus links to every **solution** notebook (HTML + source)  
- **[Lab exercises](exercises/index.qmd)** — microbiome afternoon tasks (Day 1, 2, 4) with lecture placement hints  
- No grade on this site: try the tasks yourself first; solutions are there when you need them.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
<img src="assets/hub-icons/book.svg" class="hub-inline-icon" alt="" aria-hidden="true" /> Outline & reading companion
:::
### If you want more structure

- **[Curriculum outline](curriculum-outline.qmd)** — how the three days connect (also useful if you miss a session)  
- **[Reading companion](modules/index.qmd)** — eight chapter pages that explain the ideas in prose, with light code and links back to slides, labs, and notebooks.  
:::
:::

::: {.hub-week}
## Your week at a glance

::: {.hub-week-intro}
Think of it as one story in three chapters: **see the pattern**, **keep your work honest**, then **choose and explain** models without pretending they’re magic. Curiosity beats perfection.
:::

- **Day 1 (Monday)** — predictions, when a model is “too wiggly,” and gentle introductions to linear models, logistic regression, and a bit of regularization. You’ll see **simulated genes** (we scripted the biology so we know what’s true) and **Palmer Penguins** (real measurements) side by side.  
- **Day 2 (Tuesday)** — decision trees (“if bill length is above this line, go left…”) and how to **fit and check models in a tidy, repeatable way** in R so Day 4 still makes sense.  
- **Day 3 (Wednesday)** — mini symposium and social event (no lecture deck on this site).  
- **Day 4 (Thursday)** — stronger models, what to watch when outcomes aren’t balanced, and how to discuss **why** a model leaned one way — without claiming it “knows” biology or medicine by itself.
:::

::: {.hub-week}
## Organizers & people

::: {.hub-week-intro}
This summer school is hosted by the **[Institute of Bioinformatics (IBU)](https://www.bioinformatics.unibe.ch)** at the University of Bern. 
:::

### Host institute

- **[Institute of Bioinformatics (IBU)](https://www.bioinformatics.unibe.ch)** — University of Bern

### Organizers

- **[David Miguel Francisco Ferreira](https://www.bioinformatics.unibe.ch/about_us/staff/dr_francisco_ferreira_david_miguel/index_eng.html)** — IBU · [david.francisco@unibe.ch](mailto:david.francisco@unibe.ch)  
- **[Stefan Daniel](https://www.bioinformatics.unibe.ch/about_us/staff/daniel_stefan/index_eng.html)** — IBU · [stefan.daniel@unibe.ch](mailto:stefan.daniel@unibe.ch)  

### Lecturers

- **[Aparna Pandey](https://www.bioinformatics.unibe.ch/about_us/staff/dr_pandey_aparna/index_eng.html)** — IBU · [aparna.pandey@unibe.ch](mailto:aparna.pandey@unibe.ch)  
- **[Stephan Peischl](https://www.bioinformatics.unibe.ch/about_us/staff/dr_peischl_stephan/index_eng.html)** — [Peischl Lab](https://peischllab.github.io), IBU · [stephan.peischl@unibe.ch](mailto:stephan.peischl@unibe.ch)  

### Teaching assistants

- **[Sara Krättli](https://www.bioinformatics.unibe.ch/about_us/staff/kraettli_sara/index_eng.html)** — IBU · [sara.kraettli2@unibe.ch](mailto:sara.kraettli2@unibe.ch)  
- **[Karolina Wachala](https://www.bioinformatics.unibe.ch/about_us/staff/wachaa_karolina/index_eng.html)** — Peischl Lab, IBU · [karolina.wachala@unibe.ch](mailto:karolina.wachala@unibe.ch)  
- **[Julio Ayala](https://www.thee.iee.unibe.ch/about_us/team/staff/ayala_julio/index_eng.html)** — Institute of Ecology and Evolution (IEE), University of Bern · [julio.ayalalopez@unibe.ch](mailto:julio.ayalalopez@unibe.ch)  

### Funding

- **[CUSO](https://deutsch.cuso.ch/willkommen)** — Conférence universitaire de Suisse occidentale (doctoral programme)
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="simple"}
## For instructors & TAs

- **Local preview:** from this project folder run `quarto preview` so links resolve; after `quarto render`, you can serve `_site/` with any static file server.
- **Slide decks (Reveal.js):** use the navbar Day links or open `_site/slides/day-02-tuesday.html` after render. IDE preview of a `.qmd` file shows a scrolling article — that is not the slide deck. To preview one deck: `quarto preview slides/day-02-tuesday.html`.  

- **Course design detail** — the [curriculum outline](curriculum-outline.qmd) describes the two-track story (genes + Palmer Penguins), calibration, causal guardrails around explainability, data/model cards, reproducibility, and assessment prompts.  
- **Site output** lives in `_site/`; GitHub Actions builds the same way for Pages.  
- **Palmer Penguins** — [Data card](data/cards/palmer-penguins.qmd) and [full catalog](data/index.qmd); Day 2 (Tuesday) **modeling pipeline** examples use the same **Adelie vs Gentoo** setup as the penguin classification notebook (implemented with the `tidymodels` R packages).  

- **Single source for the canonical penguin pipeline** — [`_includes/day02-tidymodels-walkthrough.qmd`](_includes/day02-tidymodels-walkthrough.qmd) (Day 2 slides + Chapter 4 via `tidymodels-pipeline.qmd` wrapper).
- **Slide reveal mode** — edit `incremental` in [`slides/_reveal-config.yml`](slides/_reveal-config.yml) (`false` = full slide at once; `true` = step-by-step bullets), then re-render the day deck.
- **Reading chapter (L1/L2, lasso, elastic net)** — [Chapter 2](modules/module-02-regularization.qmd).
:::
